evals.name: doc-driven-onboarding
evals.prompt: Onboard the client from this proposal: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wV3CGVM_VhlZEc9y9rztm445G8Xse3IEVrW0yhcLHD0/edit. Their contact email is jordan@scalelogix.ai.
evals.expected_output: Skill calls extract_proposal.py with the doc ID, parses out client/scope/project type, builds the full spec (doc + checklist + email), and pipes to create_onboarding.py. Final report includes 4 URLs (folder, doc, sheet) and confirms Gmail draft saved.

evals.name: inline-intake-web-design
evals.prompt: Set up onboarding for Acme Corp. Web design project, 4 weeks, $5K, contact is Sarah Chen at sarah@acmecorp.com. Scope is a 5-page marketing site rebuild with a booking integration.
evals.expected_output: Skill skips the doc extraction (no URL given), uses the provided fields, picks the web-design checklist template, generates an onboarding doc with project overview / scope / timeline / comms / what-we-need / next-steps, generates checklist with web-design stages plus booking-integration row in Build, and drafts a sub-200-word welcome email with no em dashes.

evals.name: sparse-input-clarification
evals.prompt: We just signed FintechCo for an AI automation build.
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes inputs are insufficient (no contact email, no scope detail) and asks 4-5 targeted intake questions before doing anything. Does NOT generate placeholder content or guess.
